what: A Library of Awesome: a growing, static, agent-contributable catalog of buildable use-cases for the RAPP static-API stack.
how_agents_use_this: Match a need to a scenario via 'when_to_use', then read its data_url for the record and demo_url for the working page. Contribute new scenarios per llms.txt.

stack./track: QR -> hash-verified 'extract' cell runs in the scanner's browser, fetches a free live source, renders one value. No server.
stack./fn: verify-before-exec compute cells: fetch bytes, recompute SHA-256, refuse on mismatch, import exact bytes, run offscreen in a sandboxed iframe.
stack./vbrainstem-cell: an entire CPython 3.12 (Pyodide/WASM) boots headless in an opaque-origin sandboxed iframe. Zero install, no server, no tab.
stack./mcp: a static MCP server (Node stdio shim, zero per-tool logic; each tool = a pinned verified cell). Any LLM speaks to it with no browser.
stack./resolver: short-id ([owner/]name[/subpath][@sha8]) -> full constellation URL; the host lives in exactly one line.
stack.rapp-twin.profile.md: the two-sided digital twin (god on-device lead / dog public bones), sealed privacy, Dream Catcher merge — nothing is lost.
stack.loop.prompt.md: a paste-over-anything improvement ratchet that discovers its own score and keeps only verified wins.

channels.title: Physical World
channels.blurb: QR on paper, objects, stickers, skin, stone — the physical thing runs live code inside whoever looks at it.

channels.title: Verify-Before-Exec
channels.blurb: Tamper-evident, un-killable, trust-the-hash-not-the-host. Run untrusted-CDN code and prove it wasn't changed.

channels.title: Agent to Agent
channels.blurb: MCP tools, brainstems as bytecode, signed dead-drops — machines using the stack with no human and no browser.

channels.title: Human & Personal
channels.blurb: Twins, gifts, memorials, time capsules, dead-man switches — the emotional edge of a serverless, forever stack.

channels.title: Self-Building
channels.blurb: The loop, the tumbler, systems that improve and verify themselves — including this library.

channels.title: Wildcard
channels.blurb: The strange stacks that shouldn't work but provably do. Go weird, stay real.

scenarios.title: Broadcast the Secret
scenarios.when_to_use: When data must be published to a fully public, permanent store yet remain readable only by a key-holder — privacy as a seal, not a location.

scenarios.title: Kill the Host
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need an endpoint that survives its origin being deleted — resolve+verify the same content from any mirror.

scenarios.title: One String, Two Universes
scenarios.when_to_use: When the same verified capability must be callable by an AI (MCP, no browser) and a human (QR, no install) with identical trust.

scenarios.title: Taste the Weather
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: live external data rendered client-side from a scannable/shareable trigger, with no backend and a tamper-proof compute step.

scenarios.title: The Byte Trap
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need to run code fetched from an untrusted host and prove byte-for-byte it wasn't altered before it executes.

scenarios.title: The Quine Ratchet
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a domain-agnostic improvement loop that discovers its own score, keeps only verified gains, and stops itself at genuine human-decision boundaries.

scenarios.title: The Reunion Where Two Memories Fuse
scenarios.when_to_use: When two datasets must merge without ever losing or overwriting a record — conflicts preserved as alternate frames, append-only.

scenarios.title: Photosynthesis
scenarios.when_to_use: When a client-side artifact should both consume live data and emit a new shareable trigger, with zero backend.

scenarios.title: The Immortal Committer
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a permanent physical marker that renders a live, ever-updating public number with no server to maintain.

scenarios.title: The Paper That Boots Python
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a full language runtime on a device with nothing installed, delivered by a scannable trigger.

scenarios.title: Proof of Freshness
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need to prove a displayed live value was fetched now (not staged) and bind it to a browser-verifiable hash of the exact response bytes.

scenarios.title: The Unbreakable Promise
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: a tamper-evident personal commitment that anyone can re-verify offline from shared text + key + signature.

scenarios.title: Living Leaderboard
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: a physical trigger that always resolves to a fresh ranked list by fetching public data in-browser with verify-before-exec.

scenarios.title: Only in the Same Frame
scenarios.when_to_use: When a secret must have NO location until two independently-held carriers meet: XOR-split an AES-256 key into 2-of-2 shares, put {ciphertext + share A} on one carrier and {share B} on the other, and gate every read on physical co-presence — neither share alone leaks a bit, and a hash-verified /fn cell reassembles the key and decrypts entirely client-side, so no server ever holds a half or witnesses the join.

scenarios.title: The Artifact With a Signed Bloodline
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need tamper-evident provenance / version history with no server or ledger: have each new state sign SHA-256(state) ‖ the previous signature, persist the append-only chain locally, and let any holder of the public key walk it to pinpoint the exact generation where the lineage breaks — and re-verify a shared head from a QR alone.

scenarios.title: The Brainstem Baton
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need: to hand a running agent's live state from one machine/process to another through a single static, content-addressed, sealed file — pause on host A, resume on host B from the exact step, with a hash proving the state wasn't altered in transit and a key gating who may read it.

scenarios.title: The Fast-Forward Parliament
scenarios.when_to_use: When N autonomous agents must agree on one value (or a canonical order) with no coordinator, no quorum math, and no vote tally: model the decision as an atomic compare-and-swap on one shared ref (a git fast-forward-only push, or a native atomic transaction) — every agent bases on the same parent, exactly one write lands per parent, and the accepted ref IS the decision.

scenarios.title: The Test That Breeds
scenarios.when_to_use: When a client-side artifact should discover a fact by running code (a property-based fuzzer that finds + minimizes a counterexample), freeze that finding into a content-addressed, self-contained payload, and emit a new scannable trigger that re-verifies and re-executes the exact finding on any other device — a compute cell whose output is the next compute cell, with no backend.

scenarios.title: The Unriggable Ticket
scenarios.when_to_use: When you need a tamper-proof outcome that no party can bias or predict and no server hosts: commit the inputs up front (printed / in a link), bind the result to a FUTURE public randomness beacon (drand League of Entropy), and let the verifier recompute it byte-for-byte in their own browser through a hash-pinned cell. Reusable for provably-fair draws, unriggable raffles, commit-reveal, fair coin-flips, and any 'trust me' that should be 'verify me instead'.
